This one could use the most review as I haven't gotten the test environment re-setup yet.

Patch Set 3:

This patch looks like a dup effort to http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/8116. I'll restore 8116 for further review. Since i have running it a long time when i test 3.12 kernel with ldiskfs. So i think it should be consider a candidate. though exist some worry that it may hurt performance.

Patch Set 3:

Yeah, it covers the same ground. I think the difference may be that my version already has the cleanups suggested during review with yours. I had this patch lying around from earlier work and 8816 was abandoned without comment. OTOH, yours has actually seen real testing. I already see a bug in mine that will come out during testing (first map call will be correct, subsequent ones will use the wrong length).

parents.subject: LU-4416 osd-ldiskfs: add check for 3 arg ext4_journal_start

commit.subject: LU-4416 osd-ldiskfs: add support for ext4_map_blocks
commit.message:
LU-4416 osd-ldiskfs: add support for ext4_map_blocks

Linux 2.6.35 added ext4_map_blocks but kept the extent walking
interfaces around for a while. Since Linux 3.8, those interfaces
have significantly changed and the right fix is to use
ext4_map_blocks.

Signed-off-by: Jeff Mahoney <jeffm@suse.com>
Change-Id: I6f0d0280f8cfd7fe3717f3afea45af6eb740e4fa
